serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Bobo Chicken weighs approximately 400 g, comprising poached chicken pieces dressed in spicy chilli oil sauce, typically served with vegetables or noodles.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, stewed (NDB 05013) combined with estimated sauce component from USDA SR Legacy condiment entries
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Bobo Chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for poached/stewed chicken meat (NDB 05013) with USDA SR Legacy entries for chilli oil, soy sauce, sesame oil, peanuts, and garlic to approximate the composite dish at approximately 60% chicken and 40% sauce/accompaniment by weight. Input record values (energy 162.5 kcal, protein 11.25 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 7.5 g, sodium 450 mg) are consistent with this USDA-based composite estimate and are retained with medium confidence. Potassium estimated at 250 mg/100g from USDA chicken and peanut entries.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on mixed chicken and noodle dish with chilli oil dressing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bobo Chicken. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for mixed chicken dishes with moderate carbohydrate from glass or wheat noodles (GI range 40–55). The dish is predominantly protein and fat with moderate carbohydrate (7.5 g/100g); the chilli oil dressing and vinegar component further attenuate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (45 × 30 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 13.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 450 mg/100g; estimated 1800 mg per 400 g serving, representing 78–90% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and chilli oil on the side, and limit portion size to no more than 200 g to keep sodium intake below 900 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (7.5 g/100g) with low GI (~45); high protein (11.25 g/100g) from poached chicken supports satiety and blunts postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes may consume Bobo Chicken in a standard 200–300 g portion, preferring glass noodles over wheat noodles to minimise glycaemic load, and monitoring total carbohydrate intake from accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at 2.0 g/100g; however, sodium at 450 mg/100g and total fat at 10 g/100g (predominantly from chilli oil and peanuts) require portion control
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit Bobo Chicken to a 200 g portion, request reduced chilli oil and soy sauce to lower sodium and total fat, and note that the unsaturated fat profile from sesame oil and peanuts is relatively favourable.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory ingredients (ginger, garlic, sesame oil) are beneficial; however, chilli oil (nightshade-derived) and high sodium (450 mg/100g) may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the anti-inflammatory polyphenols in ginger and garlic but should request reduced chilli oil and soy sauce, and monitor individual response to Sichuan peppercorns and nightshade-derived ingredients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bobo Chicken (钵钵鸡, Bō Bō Jī) is a traditional Sichuan street food originating from Chengdu, China, and widely served in Singapore's Chinese restaurants and hawker-style eateries. The dish consists of bite-sized pieces of poached chicken — and sometimes vegetables, tofu, or noodles — served cold and dressed in a richly aromatic sauce of chilli oil, soy sauce, sesame oil, black vinegar, Sichuan peppercorns, garlic, ginger, and crushed peanuts. The name 'bobo' refers to the clay pot (钵) traditionally used to hold the skewered ingredients in the sauce. The flavour profile is intensely savoury, spicy, numbing (from Sichuan peppercorns), and slightly tangy from the black vinegar. The texture contrasts tender, silky poached chicken with the crunch of peanuts and the slipperiness of optional glass noodles. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 162.5 kcal, 11.25 g protein, 10.0 g fat (saturated fat 2.0 g), and 7.5 g carbohydrate with a low glycaemic index of approximately 45. Sodium is high at 450 mg/100g, making it a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. The high protein content from lean poached chicken and the low GI make it relatively suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management in controlled portions. Anti-inflammatory compounds from ginger, garlic, and sesame oil provide additional functional food value. A standard serving is one bowl of approximately 400 g.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Bobo Chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for poached chicken and sauce ingredients. The input record values are consistent with this composite estimate and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite of poached chicken (NDB 05013) and sauce components (chilli oil, soy sauce, sesame oil, peanuts); no exact approved-source entry exists for Bobo Chicken as a composite dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.5) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (3.2) are estimated from USDA fatty acid profiles for chilli oil and peanuts; these are approximate. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this composite Sichuan cold chicken dish are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo as of 2024. 4. The input record incorrectly tagged this dish under 'Drinks & Beverages'; corrected to 'Mixed / Poultry dishes with sauce'. 5. country_of_origin in the input was listed as Singapore; the dish originates from Sichuan, China, and is served in Singapore — both contexts are preserved. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/trace for this preparation based on USDA data for the component ingredients; no industrially produced trans fats are present. 7. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for Bobo Chicken has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2024.
